pattern: Greedily halve the currently largest pile each of the k operations using a max-heap to track the maximum efficiently.
hints: Each operation removes floor(half) stones, so removing from the biggest pile saves the most every time.
A max-heap lets you repeatedly pull the largest pile, halve it, and push the remainder back.
Apply k pops: take the top pile p, subtract floor(p/2), push the new value, and finally sum what remains.

edge_inputs.description: more operations than needed drive a pile to 1
odd pile rounds down when halved
piles already at one cannot shrink

stress.description: many piles with a large operation count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    piles = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, min(100000, n * 14))
    return [piles, k]
stress.note: stresses repeated max extraction and halving
